pattern: The array's values are each built entirely from earlier, smaller indices, so a single left-to-right pass that fills in each rule as it goes computes the whole array -- and its maximum -- without any recursion.
hints: Every index beyond the first two is defined purely in terms of a strictly smaller index, so you can fill the array in order from the start and never need a value before it has already been computed.
Split the work by whether the current index is even or odd: an even index just copies an earlier value, while an odd index adds two earlier values together.
Build the whole array left to right using those two rules, tracking the running maximum as you go (or taking the maximum once the array is complete).

edge_inputs.description: small n beyond the statement examples
n is an exact power of two, exercising the even branch repeatedly
odd n just below the maximum

stress.description: random n across the whole allowed range
